source_inputs: RI v1 registry concepts preserved with v1 blocked status
maximum_capability_doctrine: Each FFET defines the maximum concrete capability possible inside its approved exact-file scope. No minimal, stale, placeholder, scaffold-only, or metadata-only FFET is valid unless explicitly docs/control-only.

ffets.name: NestJS 11 prerequisite re-verification before runtime wiring
ffets.maximum_concrete_capability_within_scope: Produce execution-time evidence that @nestjs/common, @nestjs/core, and @nestjs/platform-express remain on NestJS 11 and that the full pre-wiring validation ladder is green before any runtime wiring PR can start.
ffets.runtime_mcr: A committed evidence artifact records NestJS 11 package metadata and successful validation commands before any Stage 2 RI wiring FFET proceeds.
validation_commands: node -e "const p=require('./apps/api/package.json'); for (const k of ['@nestjs/common','@nestjs/core','@nestjs/platform-express']) { if (!String(p.dependencies[k]||'').startsWith('^11.')) throw new Error(`${k} is not NestJS 11`); }"
stop_conditions: Any NestJS package is not on major 11.
Validation failure cannot be repaired inside this evidence-only FFET.
Any required package or lockfile change is discovered; produce a separate dependency control PR instead of widening scope.
ffets.rollback_notes: Revert the evidence artifact only. This FFET does not change runtime, package metadata, or lockfiles.

ffets.name: Versioned contract/package namespace migration for deferred STAGE0-W3-FFET-002
ffets.maximum_concrete_capability_within_scope: Execute the semantic contract/package namespace migration for the exact deferred Stage 0 contract surface before any Stage 2 wiring consumes those identifiers.
ffets.runtime_mcr: The 11 deferred contract/package/tooling files are renamed or reconciled through a versioned contract-change review, contracts validate, and downstream RI consumers can import the post-migration names without preserving AKTI-era semantic identifiers.
stop_conditions: A contract identifier change requires API, schema, route, permission, or business-rule decisions not present in committed sources.
Any consumer must be edited outside the approved contract-change scope before the migration can validate.
A generated artifact would need manual editing instead of generator output.
ffets.rollback_notes: Revert the versioned contract-change PR before runtime RI consumers are merged. Do not partially revert after consumers are wired.

allowed: TypeScript import/export mismatch inside owned contract files
local test expectation drift caused by the contract rename

expected_evidence: consumer readiness notes for dependent RI FFETs

implications.gatekeeper: Contract identifiers must be stable before Gatekeeper route/action gates bind to them.
implications.foundry: Foundry activation manifests must consume post-migration identifiers.
implications.audit: Audit evidence names must not straddle old and new semantic contract identifiers.
implications.tenant_isolation: No tenant isolation change is authorized by this contract-change FFET.

ffets.name: Rebased Mount 6A-6C backend runtime modules
ffets.maximum_concrete_capability_within_scope: Execute the rebased runtime integration capability from RI v1 after Stage 1 amendment artifacts, NestJS 11 re-verification, and the Stage 0 contract-change prerequisite are satisfied. Scope is limited to the exact files listed here.
ffets.runtime_mcr: Nest application can import Phase 6A, 6B, and 6C runtime modules without bootstrap errors while preserving existing module boundaries.
stop_conditions: required edit outside exact files
schema/migration/package/generated/script change required
new API/permission/business/ADL decision required
frontend screen contract missing for frontend work
validation failure after 3 self-heal attempts
Stage 1 amendment artifacts are stale or not represented in this FFET.
STAGE0-W3-FFET-002 has not landed before runtime consumers need renamed contract identifiers.
Runtime wiring requires schema/package/generated/frontend work outside this FFET scope.
ffets.rollback_notes: Revert the single FFET PR; no shared migration or schema rollback is authorized inside RI-FFET scope.

self_heal_policy.allowed: deterministic repairs inside exact files only
self_heal_policy.forbidden: scope expansion, weakened tests, invented doctrine, schema/package/generated/script changes

implications.gatekeeper: Must not bypass Gatekeeper where the wired action is high-risk; explicit gate proof belongs to dependent gatekeeper/demo FFETs.
implications.foundry: Must respect Foundry activation state when exposed through dependent module/frontend wiring.
implications.audit: Must not suppress evidence obligations; audit proof belongs to dependent audit/demo FFETs.
implications.tenant_isolation: Must preserve tenant/org neutrality and rely on tenant-isolation FFETs for negative proof.
